hero.question: Los Angeles Angels vs. Cincinnati Reds
hero.prob_take: A 60.5% win probability for the Angels assumes they're the clear favorites, but a 23-point surge in 24 hours signals either sharp money reacting to lineup news or retail chasing momentum on a team that hasn't earned this confidence yet. The Reds have been undervalued in close matchups all season, and unless the Angels just got a star player back healthy, this feels like a classic overreaction to a single win or favorable odds movement.

daily_take.headline: Angels Rally Priced Like It Already Happened. Smart Money Disagrees.
daily_take.deck: Angels futures jumped 23 points to 60.5% on momentum alone, but the market is pricing in a clean sweep against Cincinnati. This is a classic "result-dependent" move where bettors chase the narrative instead of the math. A 60%+ favorite in a single game is aggressive given the Reds' recent form and pitching matchup. Fade the hype, buy the Reds.
